scenario: An IDP Application extracts data from invoices. Each extracted field comes with a confidence score (0.0 to 1.0). The team wants a downstream gateway to route to human review if ANY field has confidence below 0.85, otherwise continue automated processing.
question: Which BPMN routing approach fits?

options.text: Configure the IDP Application itself to automatically route to human review when any extracted field falls below the confidence threshold -- IDP does not provide a built-in routing decision knob of this kind. IDP is responsible for document extraction and confidence scoring; it produces results and scores but does not orchestrate the downstream business process flow. Routing logic -- including confidence threshold checks -- belongs in the BPMN model using gateway conditions. Embedding routing decisions inside IDP would tightly couple the extraction tool to business rules and make process changes harder.

Set a global minConfidence threshold attribute on the IDP Application configuration, so IDP automatically fails or reroutes when any field falls below that threshold. While IDP may surface per-field confidence scores, a single global threshold attribute that auto-fails the extraction is not the design pattern. 80), and the threshold logic belongs in the BPMN gateway conditions where it can be business-rule-driven and separately maintainable.
85 for the automated branch (true = all high confidence), default flow → human review. 85 true → human review. md:22 ("Receive confidence scores for each extracted value").

FEEL expressions cannot check per-field confidence values -- there is no FEEL syntax capable of iterating over a collection of fields and checking a property like confidence score on each one. This claim is inaccurate. FEEL includes powerful quantified expressions: the every quantifier checks whether a predicate holds for all items in a collection, and the some quantifier checks whether it holds for at least one item. Applied to a list of extracted fields, these quantifiers directly express the "any field below threshold" requirement without custom code or external routing tools.

a.text: Incorrect. IDP exposes extracted fields with per-field confidence in the result. FEEL's every (universal quantifier) and some (existential quantifier) operate on lists/collections. For the "any field below threshold → human review" requirement, the natural expression is either every confidence >= threshold (for the all-good path) or some confidence < threshold (for the needs-review path). Use one or the other on the Exclusive Gateway's outgoing arrows.

b.text: Incorrect. IDP's job is extraction, not orchestration. Routing decisions belong in BPMN. (Some IDP integrations may bundle simple rules, but the canonical separation is: IDP extracts, BPMN routes.)

c.text: Correct. Even if you could set a global confidence threshold in IDP, the team often wants different thresholds per field (vendorName requires 0.9, invoiceDate requires 0.85). BPMN-level routing is more flexible.

d.text: Incorrect. FEEL's quantifiers (every, some, any) work over lists.

explanation: Even if you could set a global confidence threshold in IDP, the team often wants different thresholds per field (vendorName requires 0.9, invoiceDate requires 0.85). BPMN-level routing is more flexible.
